# IDE Integration for Spec-Driven Workflows
<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

## TL;DR

Spec-driven development (SDD) tooling matured rapidly in 2025-2026. Three dominant approaches exist: (1) **skill/plugin frameworks** that enforce structured workflows within AI coding agents (Superpowers, Spec Kit), (2) **dedicated spec-first IDEs** (Kiro), and (3) **cross-tool configuration standards** (AGENTS.md, Cursor Rules). For estimation purposes, SDD tooling shifts effort from coding to specification — reinforcing the finding that coding is only 16-39% of development time and that upfront planning quality determines AI productivity outcomes.

---

## 1. Claude Code Skills for Spec Writing

### Superpowers (obra/superpowers)

**What it does:** A complete software development workflow framework that enforces structured phases: brainstorm, design spec, implementation plan, subagent-driven execution, code review, and merge. The brainstorming skill forces collaborative questioning to refine vague requests into fully-formed design specifications before any code is written.

**How it integrates:** Claude Code plugin. Install via `/plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace`. Primary commands: `/superpowers:brainstorm`, `/superpowers:write-plan`, `/superpowers:execute-plan`. Over 42,000 GitHub stars; accepted into the Anthropic marketplace January 2026.

**Strengths:**
- Enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR TDD cycle — deletes code written before tests
- Code review skill blocks progress on critical issues
- Two-tier architecture: core skills + personal skills in `~/.config/superpowers/skills/`
- Brainstorming phase produces specs in digestible chunks for human review

**Limitations:**
- Claude Code only (not cross-tool)
- Opinionated workflow may conflict with existing team processes
- Heavy context consumption from multi-phase pipeline

**Estimation relevance:** Directly increases specification time while reducing rework. The enforced brainstorm-then-plan pattern maps to the finding that context quality drives 40-75 pp productivity swings.

**Sources:**
- [GitHub - obra/superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers)
- [Superpowers blog post](https://blog.fsck.com/2025/10/09/superpowers/)
- [Composio: Top Claude Code Skills](https://composio.dev/content/top-claude-skills)

### Claude Code Skills Ecosystem (General)

As of March 2026, the [anthropics/skills](https://github.com/anthropics/skills) repository hosts 334+ skills (up from ~50 in mid-2025) with 87,000+ stars. Skills are SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter, optionally backed by scripts. The same skill format works across Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and Antigravity IDE.

Key authoring principles for spec-related skills:
- The `description` field is a **trigger**, not a summary — write it for model recognition
- Use Progressive Disclosure — show just enough for the agent to decide, then reveal detail
- `context: fork` runs skills in isolated subagents, keeping the main context clean

**Sources:**
- [Extend Claude with skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills)
- [Skill authoring best practices](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/best-practices)
- [Best Claude Code Skills 2026](https://www.openaitoolshub.org/en/blog/best-claude-code-skills-2026)

### levnikolaevich/claude-code-skills

A plugin suite covering the full delivery lifecycle: project bootstrap, documentation generation, codebase audits (security, quality, architecture, tests), Agile pipeline with multi-model AI review and quality gates, performance optimization, and GitHub community workflows.

**Source:** [GitHub - levnikolaevich/claude-code-skills](https://github.com/levnikolaevich/claude-code-skills)

## 2. CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md Generators and Best Practices

### CLAUDE.md

**What it does:** Project-level memory file read at the start of every Claude Code session. The `/init` command generates a starter file from project structure.

**Best practices:**
- **Delete most of what `/init` generates** — default content states the obvious and wastes context
- Target under 300 lines; every line competes with actual work for context window attention
- Document commands and workflows (prevents hallucinated flags/commands)
- Build organically from session learnings, not upfront
- Use sub-agents in `.claude/agents/` for specialized tasks with isolated context

**Limitation:** Claude-only. Teams using multiple tools need AGENTS.md.

**Sources:**
- [Writing a good CLAUDE.md](https://www.humanlayer.dev/blog/writing-a-good-claude-md)
- [Best Practices for Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices)
- [Creating the Perfect CLAUDE.md](https://dometrain.com/blog/creating-the-perfect-claudemd-for-claude-code/)

### AGENTS.md (Cross-Tool Standard)

**What it does:** A plain Markdown file providing AI coding agents with project-specific context. One file, any agent. Governed by the Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation, co-developed by Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, and Sourcegraph.

**Supported tools (as of early 2026):** Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Aider, Zed, Warp, RooCode, Codex, Jules, and others.

**How it integrates:**
- Drop `AGENTS.md` in project root (or nested directories for monorepo specificity)
- Closest AGENTS.md to the edited file takes priority
- Explicit user prompts override everything
- For Copilot in VS Code: set `"chat.useAgentsMdFile": true`

**Recommended multi-tool setup:** Write 80% of common rules in AGENTS.md, keep tool-specific behaviors in tool-specific files (.cursor/rules/, CLAUDE.md supplements). Symlink where feasible.

**Estimation relevance:** Reduces per-tool configuration overhead. One-time investment in AGENTS.md amortizes across all AI tools a team uses.

**Sources:**
- [AGENTS.md official site](https://agents.md/)
- [Complete Guide to CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md](https://medium.com/data-science-collective/the-complete-guide-to-ai-agent-memory-files-claude-md-agents-md-and-beyond-49ea0df5c5a9)
- [AGENTS.md Cross-Tool Guide](https://smartscope.blog/en/generative-ai/github-copilot/github-copilot-agents-md-guide/)

---

## 3. Cursor Rules for Spec-Driven Workflows

### .mdc Files (Current Standard)

**What it does:** Individual rule files in `.cursor/rules/` that control how Cursor's AI behaves. Replaced the deprecated `.cursorrules` root file (still supported but migration recommended). Each `.mdc` file has metadata fields:
- `description` — When Cursor should apply the rule
- `globs` — File/directory patterns (Minimatch syntax)
- `alwaysApply` — Whether to apply regardless of file context

**Rule types and priority:**
1. **Local/Manual** — Explicitly invoked with `@ruleName`
2. **Auto Attached** — Activated when matching glob patterns
3. **Agent Requested** — Agent selects based on description

**Spec-driven best practices:**
- One concern per rule, small and actionable
- Split large specs into composable `.mdc` files (reduces token usage by activating only relevant rules)
- Anchor with concrete code samples and explicit globs
- Write like internal docs with clear do/don't instructions
- Nest rules in subdirectories for monorepo specificity

**Strengths:**
- Granular control over which rules apply to which files
- Team-shared rules ensure consistent AI behavior
- Token-efficient: only relevant rules consume context

**Limitations:**
- Cursor-specific format (though AGENTS.md bridges some of this)
- Rule authoring requires understanding of how the model selects rules
- No built-in spec workflow — rules guide behavior but don't enforce phases

**Estimation relevance:** Well-crafted rules reduce correction cycles. Rules can encode estimation-relevant constraints (e.g., "always write tests before implementation", "generate acceptance criteria before coding").

**Sources:**
- [Cursor Rules Documentation](https://docs.cursor.com/context/rules)
- [Complete Cursor Rules Guide 2026](https://www.agentrulegen.com/guides/cursor-rules-guide)
- [Cursor Forum MDC Best Practices](https://forum.cursor.com/t/my-best-practices-for-mdc-rules-and-troubleshooting/50526)
- [cursorrules.org Generator](https://cursorrules.org/)

## 4. MCP Servers Relevant to Specification

### spec-workflow-mcp (@pimzino/spec-workflow-mcp)

**What it does:** An MCP server providing structured spec-driven development workflow tools with a real-time web dashboard and VSCode extension. Enforces sequential spec creation: Requirements, Design, Tasks. Includes approval workflows with revisions, task progress tracking, and multi-language support (11 languages).

**How it integrates:** Any MCP-compatible client (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.). Provides both an MCP server interface and a VSCode extension for monitoring.

**Strengths:**
- Visual dashboard for tracking spec/task progress
- Approval gates mirror traditional SDLC
- Works across multiple AI tools via MCP

**Limitations:**
- Early-stage project; community adoption unclear
- Additional infrastructure (dashboard server) adds complexity

**Source:** [GitHub - Pimzino/spec-workflow-mcp](https://github.com/Pimzino/spec-workflow-mcp)

### mcp-server-spec-driven-development

**What it does:** MCP service supporting the spec-driven development process using EARS (Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax) format. Guides developers through requirements, design documents, and code implementation via structured prompts.

**How it integrates:** Standard MCP server protocol; works with any MCP-compatible client.

**Source:** [MCP Protocol Enables Spec-Driven Development](https://mcp.aibase.com/server/1482472329346031674)

### MCP Ecosystem Context (2026)

The MCP ecosystem has grown to 1,000+ servers. Key developments relevant to spec workflows:
- **MCP Registry** (September 2025): Open catalog and API for indexing/discovery of MCP servers
- **November 2025 Spec (V2)**: Adds asynchronous execution, enterprise authorization, server discovery via `.well-known` URLs
- **Donated to Linux Foundation** (December 2025)
- Natively supported by Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Microsoft

**Sources:**
- [MCP Specification 2025-11-25](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25)
- [Best MCP Servers for Developers 2026](https://www.builder.io/blog/best-mcp-servers-2026)

## 5. GitHub Spec Kit Slash Commands

### What It Is

[Spec Kit](https://github.com/github/spec-kit) is GitHub's open-source toolkit for spec-driven development. It installs slash commands into your AI coding agent and enforces a sequential workflow: Constitution, Specify, Plan, Tasks, Implement.

### Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/speckit.constitution` | Define immutable high-level principles applied to every change |
| `/speckit.specify` | Describe *what* to build and *why* (not how); generates specification |
| `/speckit.clarify` | Resolve ambiguities in the specification before planning |
| `/speckit.plan` | Translate spec into concrete architecture, components, dependencies |
| `/speckit.tasks` | Break plan into actionable, ordered implementation checklist |
| `/speckit.implement` | Execute all tasks according to the plan |

### How It Integrates

After `specify init`, command files are placed in the agent's command directory:
- Claude Code: `.claude/commands/`
- GitHub Copilot: `.github/copilot-instructions/`

**Supported agents:** Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Cursor Agent, Qwen, OpenCode, Codex, Windsurf, Junie, KiloCode, Roo, Amp, Kiro CLI, and 15+ others.

Output artifacts live in the `.specify/` folder: spec templates, technical plans, task lists, and platform scripts.

### Strengths
- Agent-agnostic — works with virtually every major AI coding tool
- Enforces sequential discipline (can't plan before specifying)
- Constitution concept provides persistent guardrails across all changes
- Open source, backed by GitHub/Microsoft

### Limitations
- Community reports friction: "a sea of markdown documents, long agent run-times"
- Iterating on specs after plan/tasks are generated causes confusion (agent tries to create new branches)
- Heavy markdown output can overwhelm context windows
- Sequential enforcement is rigid — no easy way to jump between phases

### Estimation Relevance

Spec Kit directly maps the estimation problem: it makes the specify-plan-task breakdown explicit and traceable. The sequential workflow forces teams to acknowledge that specification time is non-trivial. However, the reported friction with iteration suggests that real-world spec work is less linear than the tool assumes.

**Sources:**
- [GitHub - github/spec-kit](https://github.com/github/spec-kit)
- [Diving Into Spec-Driven Development With Spec Kit](https://developer.microsoft.com/blog/spec-driven-development-spec-kit)
- [Deep Dive into SpecKit](https://blog.lpains.net/posts/2025-12-07-deep-dive-into-speckit/)
- [speckit Commands - DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/github/spec-kit/5-speckit-commands)
- [Putting Spec Kit Through Its Paces](https://blog.scottlogic.com/2025/11/26/putting-spec-kit-through-its-paces-radical-idea-or-reinvented-waterfall.html)

## 6. Other IDE Plugins Supporting Spec-First Development

### Kiro (AWS)

**What it does:** A dedicated spec-first agentic IDE from Amazon, built as a VS Code fork powered by Claude (via Amazon Bedrock). Enforces a three-phase specification workflow before any code generation:

1. **requirements.md** — User stories and acceptance criteria in EARS format
2. **design.md** — Technical architecture, components, data models, interfaces
3. **tasks.md** — Ordered implementation checklist

**Key features:**
- **Agent Hooks** — Event-driven automations that trigger on file save/create/delete (like an experienced dev catching things you miss)
- **Steering Files** — Set standards for code generation (tech stack, naming conventions, structure)
- **Native MCP integration** including remote servers
- **Models:** Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Haiku 4.5, DeepSeek, Qwen (all via Bedrock)

**Pricing:** Free during preview; then free tier (50 interactions/month), Pro ($19/month, 1000 interactions), Pro+ ($39/month, 3000 interactions).

**Strengths:**
- Most structured spec enforcement of any IDE — specification is not optional
- EARS-based requirements are a genuine productivity multiplier for complex systems
- Agent hooks automate boilerplate and catch mistakes in background

**Limitations:**
- AWS-only inference (Bedrock); no model BYOK
- VS Code fork means separate IDE from existing Cursor/VS Code workflows
- Currently in preview; production readiness unclear
- Closed ecosystem compared to Spec Kit's agent-agnostic approach

**Estimation relevance:** Kiro validates the SDD thesis at the IDE level. Delta Airlines reported 1,948% growth in AI coding adoption after adopting spec-driven workflows. Kiro's rigid spec-before-code enforcement makes specification effort visible and measurable — directly useful for estimation.

**Sources:**
- [Kiro Specs Documentation](https://kiro.dev/docs/specs/)
- [Kiro official site](https://kiro.dev/)
- [AWS Kiro review - The New Stack](https://thenewstack.io/aws-kiro-testing-an-ai-ide-with-a-spec-driven-approach/)
- [Kiro vs Cursor comparison](https://www.augmentcode.com/tools/kiro-vs-cursor)

### Tessl Framework

**What it does:** CLI + MCP server that creates workspace and config structure for spec-driven development across multiple coding assistants. Pursues the most ambitious SDD level: **spec-as-source**, where developers maintain only specs and code is marked `// GENERATED FROM SPEC - DO NOT EDIT`.

**Key features:**
- Reverse-engineers specs from existing code
- Maintains 1:1 mapping between spec files and code files
- **Tessl Spec Registry**: 10,000+ specs explaining how to use external libraries (prevents API hallucinations)
- Distributed as both CLI and MCP server

**Strengths:**
- Most ambitious vision: specs become the primary artifact, code is derived
- Registry addresses the library hallucination problem
- Works across multiple coding assistants

**Limitations:**
- Still in closed beta as of early 2026
- Spec-as-source is unproven at scale
- Reverse-engineering specs from existing code is inherently lossy

**Estimation relevance:** If spec-as-source works, it fundamentally changes what gets estimated — specification becomes the deliverable, not code. The Spec Registry reduces estimation uncertainty from library integration tasks.

**Sources:**
- [Martin Fowler: SDD Tools - Kiro, Spec-kit, and Tessl](https://martinfowler.com/articles/exploring-gen-ai/sdd-3-tools.html)
- [Tessl SDD Documentation](https://docs.tessl.io/use/spec-driven-development-with-tessl)
- [Tessl official site](https://tessl.io/)

### Heeki Park's Spec-Driven Approach (Claude Code Pattern)

A practitioner-documented pattern for using spec-driven development with Claude Code without additional tooling. Key insight: "Time spent in upfront planning pays dividends for implementation efficiency and output quality." Most follow-up interactions were small tweaks rather than wholesale changes when specs were written first.

**Source:** [Using spec-driven development with Claude Code](https://heeki.medium.com/using-spec-driven-development-with-claude-code-4a1ebe5d9f29)

## Comparison Matrix

| Tool | IDE Support | Spec Enforcement | Cross-Tool | Maturity | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Superpowers** | Claude Code | Strong (brainstorm-plan-TDD) | No | Production | Free/OSS |
| **Spec Kit** | 15+ agents | Sequential phases | Yes | Production | Free/OSS |
| **Kiro** | Kiro IDE only | Mandatory 3-file spec | No | Preview | Free-$39/mo |
| **Tessl** | Multi-agent CLI | Spec-as-source | Yes | Closed Beta | TBD |
| **AGENTS.md** | All major tools | Guidelines only | Yes | Standard | Free |
| **Cursor Rules** | Cursor only | Configurable | No | Production | Included |
| **spec-workflow-mcp** | MCP clients | Approval gates | Yes | Early | Free/OSS |

---

## Implications for Estimation

1. **SDD makes specification effort visible.** All these tools create explicit artifacts (requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md) that can be measured and estimated separately from coding.

2. **Specification time is non-trivial.** Spec Kit, Kiro, and Superpowers all enforce multi-phase workflows. The friction reported by users ("long agent run-times", "sea of markdown") confirms that good specs take real time — consistent with the finding that coding is only 16-39% of development effort.

3. **Tool overhead is real but amortizing.** Initial setup (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, Cursor Rules, Spec Kit constitution) is a fixed cost. The 2026 convergence on AGENTS.md reduces multi-tool configuration overhead.

4. **Context quality is the bottleneck.** Every tool in this landscape is fundamentally about improving context quality for the AI agent — through specifications, rules, skills, or memory files. This reinforces the 40-75 pp productivity swing driven by context quality.

5. **Iteration friction is the main risk.** Linear spec-plan-task workflows break down when requirements change mid-implementation. Estimation models should account for iteration cycles, not just the happy path.

6. **Three SDD maturity levels matter for estimation:**
   - **Spec-first** (Spec Kit, Superpowers): Spec written before code, but code is the artifact maintained
   - **Spec-anchored** (Kiro): Spec and code co-evolve with bidirectional traceability
   - **Spec-as-source** (Tessl): Spec is the primary artifact; code is generated — estimation shifts entirely to specification effort
